query: NgRx selectors memoization and derived state performance interview question

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this Angular, RxJS, or NgRx prompt, so broader Angular question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: NgRx Selector Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This is the official NgRx reference for selector composition, memoization behavior, and feature-state read patterns.
theirStrengths: It is the canonical source for createSelector, selector composition, and official memoization semantics.
theirGaps: It is reference-oriented and less compressed around the interview distinction between raw getters and memoized read models.

realWorldUseCases:
Memoized read model

Selectors are not just getters. They are the memoized read layer of an NgRx app: compose feature state into a view model once, reuse the last result while inputs are unchanged, and keep transformation logic out of components. The performance value comes from derived-state reuse, not from magical rerender prevention.
followUpConfusion:
Failure pattern
  • If reducers mutate state in place, selector inputs keep the same reference and memoization expectations break.
  • Selectors work best when reducers stay immutable and composition stays small.
  • The goal is reusable derived state, not hiding bad state updates.

realWorldUseCases: The competitor covers the core concept, but our page is now more explicit about the production decision or failure mode behind this topic.
actionableExamples: The competitor still provides competitive examples, so the example bar remains tied instead of a clean sweep.
followUpConfusion: Our updated page now answers the likely follow-up confusion more directly with a sharper rule, boundary, or failure pattern.

actionableExamples: Keep one compact worked example near the top so the answer stays faster to scan than a broader reference.

competitors.winReason: The updated page now wins on production framing and follow-up confusion while the competitor remains competitive on raw examples.

competitors.label: Angular University NgRx Crash Course
competitors.selectionReason: Angular University is the strongest topic-direct tutorial competitor because it covers selectors in the broader NgRx architecture and read-model flow.
theirStrengths: It connects selectors to the broader Store, reducer, and effect architecture in a practical walkthrough.
theirGaps: It is broader on NgRx overall and less tightly focused on selector memoization as an interview question on its own.

competitors.label: Stack Overflow: Ngrx selector not triggering when the reducer alters the slice
competitors.selectionReason: This is a directly relevant community comparator because it exposes the exact memoization pitfall where mutation keeps references stable and selectors stop recomputing as expected.
theirStrengths: It is anchored in a real selector-memoization bug caused by in-place mutation.
theirGaps: It is a single debugging thread and does not explain selectors as a broader read-model layer.
